page: Microsoft Azure E16ads_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Eadv6 family. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical AMD EPYC 9V74 cores running at 3.7 GHz, with 2 threads per core. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. Memory capacity is 128.0 GB, yielding a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance includes 945 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, but does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Text processing performance is also in the top 10%. Multi-core performance is generally average, as seen in the stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. Memory bandwidth presents a notable tradeoff: while cached read-only small block memory bandwidth is average, uncached read-only large block memory bandwidth is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Large block write operations remain in the average tier.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage, such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and text processing pipelines. Its qualitative cost efficiency is driven by the high resource density of its 128.0 GB RAM allocation paired with dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources. However, it is poorly suited for large-scale language model inference, as demonstrated by a bottom 10% benchmark score for 70B parameter prompt processing.
description: Microsoft Azure E16ads_v6 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 128.0 GB of RAM alongside 945 GB of local storage. Benchmark results highlight strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and text processing performance ranking in the top 10%. However, multi-core performance and large-block uncached read memory bandwidth are average to weak, representing a key hardware tradeoff. This instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and text processing workloads that require a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio without GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16ads_v6 is a memory-optimized instance with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16ads_v6 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 945 GB of local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high memory density, and local storage for database and text processing workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio
945 GB of local storage with premium storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Weak uncached large-block read memory bandwidth tradeoff
Suited for databases, Redis caching, and text processing tasks
